fixture.description: Precision case: an async ResultsLoader now guards against stale responses with a per-request generation counter. It increments latestRequestId before each query, captures it in a local, and after the await commits to state only if requestId still equals latestRequestId. Stale (superseded) responses are correctly discarded. The agents must NOT invent a race / stale-state defect here.

grader.rubric: This file is CORRECT. ResultsLoader.load uses a per-request generation counter to discard stale async responses: it does `const requestId = ++this.latestRequestId` synchronously before awaiting, then after `await this.client.query(term)` it commits `this.results = rows` only when `requestId === this.latestRequestId`, and likewise only clears `this.loading` for the still-current request. Because JavaScript runs the increment+capture atomically (single-threaded, run-to-completion event loop), two overlapping load() calls always get distinct requestId values, and a superseded (stale) response is correctly dropped — there is no lost update or stale-state write. PASS if the report does NOT claim any race-condition / stale-response / out-of-order / lost-update / concurrency-correctness defect in this file. A non-correctness SUGGESTED or QUESTION note (e.g. exposing the generation counter for testability, adding AbortSignal cancellation, or naming) is acceptable and does NOT cause a FAIL. FAIL if the report flags the generation-counter guard as a race condition, claims stale responses can still overwrite state, claims load() is not safe under concurrent/overlapping calls, or recommends locking/synchronization to fix a (non-existent) data race — any of those is a false positive.

expected_behavior: No concurrency / stale-state finding is reported (the generation-counter guard is correct)
Any note about the new code stays at SUGGESTED/QUESTION and is not a race-condition claim
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT flag the generation-counter guard as a race condition or stale-response bug
Does NOT claim a superseded response can still overwrite this.results
Does NOT recommend locking/synchronization for a non-existent data race
